name: Tower and Cala d'en Basset via Pas d'en Grau — circular from Sant Elm
name_original: Torre y Cala d'en Basset por el Pas d'en Grau — circular desde Sant Elm
region_intro: Hiking around Andratx offers access to a diverse natural landscape in the southwest of Mallorca. The region is characterized by the foothills of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, a rugged coastline with secluded coves, and the protected island of Sa Dragonera Natural Park. These features provide a variety of terrains, from mountain trails with panoramic sea views to coastal paths and nature reserves. The area's landscape includes majestic mountains, ancient olive groves, and diverse Balearic flora. Best hiking trails in Andratx The most popular hiking route is Tower and Cala d'en Basset via Pas d'en Grau — circular from Sant Elm, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the historic tower and coastal scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Sa Dragonera – View of Sa Dragonera Island loop from Es Pantaleu, a difficult 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail provides expansive views of Sa Dragonera Island and the surrounding sea. Local hikers also love the View of Camp de Mar Bay – Caló d'en Monjo loop from Talaia del Cap Andritxol, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Andratx is defined by its Serra de Tramuntana mountain paths, rugged coastlines, and trails within Sa Dragonera Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Andratx are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8,300 reviews. More than 34,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Andratx's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect in Andratx?
A: Andratx offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from mountain paths in the foothills of the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt; with panoramic sea views, to rugged coastal walks and trails within the protected &lt;strong&gt;Sa Dragonera Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll find routes winding through ancient olive groves, lush citrus orchards, and along dramatic cliffs overlooking the Mediterranean.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Andratx region?
A: There are over 250 hiking routes in the Andratx region. These include a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring options for various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hikes in Andratx?
A: Yes, Andratx has over 100 easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed walk. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed here, many of the shorter, less strenuous paths along the coast or through the lower valleys would be ideal. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998995655/tower-and-cala-d-en-basset-via-pas-d-en-grau-circular-from-sant-elm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower and Cala d'en Basset via Pas d'en Grau — circular from Sant Elm&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.3 km trail that takes under two hours, offering coastal views and a historic tower.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking in Andratx?
A: Andratx is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can hike to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/81808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Cala en Basset&lt;/a&gt;, explore the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trapa monastery&lt;/a&gt; with stunning views over Sa Dragonera, or visit various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/465708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Andritxol&lt;/a&gt; for vistas of Camp de Mar and Peguera. The region also features unique natural formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5717057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Llagrimera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Andratx?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Andratx are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10394646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Camp de Mar Bay – Caló d'en Monjo loop from Talaia del Cap Andritxol&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.9 km route offering beautiful coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Andratx, particularly in the Serra de Tramuntana foothills, offers challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14858164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera – View of Sa Dragonera Island loop from Es Pantaleu&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.6 km path with significant elevation gain, providing expansive views of Sa Dragonera Island. Another demanding option is the 20.5 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998971537/cabo-tramuntana-cabo-llebeig-y-na-popia-the-lighthouses-of-sa-dragonera-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Tramuntana, Cabo Llebeig y na Pòpia&lt;/a&gt; route, exploring the lighthouses of Sa Dragonera Natural Park.

Q: What is Sa Dragonera Natural Park and how can I hike there?
A: Sa Dragonera is a small, protected island off the coast of Andratx, designated as a nature reserve. It's teeming with diverse flora and fauna. You can reach it by boat from Sant Elm or Port d'Andratx and explore its nature trails, hike along its cliffs, and enjoy spectacular coastal views. The island offers a unique ecosystem for hiking. A challenging route that offers views of the island is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31701868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera – View of Sa Dragonera loop from puig d'en Vic&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Andratx?
A: The hiking routes in Andratx are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8,300 reviews. Hikers often praise the breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean, the diverse landscapes ranging from mountains to secluded coves, and the well-maintained trails that allow for exploration of Balearic wildlife and ancient groves.

Q: Is Andratx a good destination for year-round hiking?
A: Yes, Andratx benefits from a climate conducive to year-round outdoor pursuits. While summers can be hot, the spring and autumn months offer ideal temperatures for hiking. Even in winter, the mild climate allows for enjoyable walks, especially along the coast or in the lower mountain areas.

Q: Are there any specific attractions near Port d'Andratx for hikers?
A: Port d'Andratx serves as an excellent base for exploring the surrounding area. From here, you can access trails leading to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trapa monastery&lt;/a&gt;, which is situated in a hidden bay and offers spectacular sea views. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/81808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Cala en Basset&lt;/a&gt; is also accessible via hikes from the Port area or Sant Elm, providing dramatic coastal scenery.

Q: Where can I find more information about activities and experiences in Andratx?
A: For a broader overview of activities and experiences beyond hiking in the region, you can visit the official tourism website: &lt;a href="https://visit-andratx.com/en/experiences-things-to-do-andratx/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visit-andratx.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: An absolute dream place on Mallorca is (in my opinion) definitely the fishing village "Sant Elm" (also called San Telmo). The place is outside the busy tourist centers at the southern end of the Tramuntana. From here you can ideally go on hikes in the mountains (such as to the monastery "La Trapa") or actively explore the area by mountain bike or kayak. In the town itself, the mood is relaxed and serene. In the restaurants you can sit fantastically close to the water and enjoy everything your heart desires, from fresh fish to tapas or almond ice cream.
Nice quiet bay as it is only accessible on foot or by boat

The 'Torre Cala d'en Basset' once protected the islanders from piracy. Today the watchtower is a meeting place for those who want to enjoy a beautiful view, to have a picnic or to take pictures. Others come to leave their graffiti marks.
